title: Home
subtitle: How to create screens with FrontX framework
overview_description: FrontX uses a screenset-based architecture. Each screenset is a self-contained module with its own screens, state, events, effects, and translations. Screens are lazy-loaded components registered in a screenset config.
step1: 1. Add a screen ID constant to the screenset ids.ts file
step2: 2. Create screen folder: screens/{name}/ with i18n/ and components/
step3: 3. Create the Screen component using useScreenTranslations and TextLoader
step4: 4. Add i18n JSON files for all supported languages with I18nRegistry.createLoader
step5: 5. Register the screen in the screenset config menu with lazy import
structure_tree:
src/screensets/{name}/
  ids.ts                    — screen ID constants
  {name}Screenset.tsx        — screenset config & registration
  i18n/                      — screenset-level translations
  screens/
    {screen}/
      {Screen}Screen.tsx     — screen component (default export)
      i18n/                  — screen-level translations
      components/            — screen-local components
rule1: Use @hai3/uikit components; local uikit only if missing from the kit.
rule2: Data flow follows event-driven architecture — actions emit events, effects update slices.
rule3: No direct slice imports in screens; use @hai3/react hooks or local actions.
rule4: All text must use t() with proper i18n namespaces: screen.{screenset}.{screen}:key.
